OBJECTIONS TO DEFT'S EVIDENCE FILED BY PLAINTIFF HELLAND, PATRICK

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PATRICK HELLAND'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF TENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED. THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED ON A PUBLIC STREET AFTER PLAINTIFF'S SHIFT ENDED AS HE WAS ATTEMPTING TO COMMUTE HOME. NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE SUBMITTED SHOWING THAT THE ACCIDENT ACTUALLY OCCURRED ON DEFENDANT'S PROPERTY. JERROLD AVENUE IS A PUBLIC THOROUGHFARE AND THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT USED THE STREET'S PARKING DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THE NECESSARY CONTROL. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THE SPECIAL RISK EXCEPTION APPLIES. NO SHOWING THAT THE "DANGER IS ONE TO WHICH THE EMPLOYEE, BY REASON OF AND IN CONNECTION WITH HIS EMPLOYMENT, IS SUBJECTED PARTICULARLY OR TO AN ABNORMAL DEGREE. NO RISK IDENTIFIED THAT IS "DISTINCTIVE IN NATURE OR QUANTITATIVELY GREATER THAN RISKS COMMON TO THE PUBLIC." GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA V. WORKERS' COMP. APPEALS BOARD (1976) 16 CAL.3D 595. THE COURT SUSTAINED PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO STANGHELLINI AND VELEZ DECLARATIONS, REMAINING OBJECTIONS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ALL OVERRULED AS TO UNDISPUTED FACTS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

SANCTIONS ORDERED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 AGAINST PETER C. LABRADOR, ESQ., FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR AT THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING ON: JAN-13-2010 PAYABLE ON OR BEFORE JAN-28-2010. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PATRICK HELLAND'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF TENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE.
APPEARANCES: David L. Fiol, Esq. of Brent & Fiol, LLP 259-4420 for pltf & moving party; C. Martin Gibson, Esq. of Gibson Law Offices 833-0166 & Peter C. Labrador, Esq. 650/347-0381 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED. THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED ON A PUBLIC STREET AFTER PLAINTIFF'S SHIFT ENDED AS HE WAS ATTEMPTING TO COMMUTE HOME. NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE SUBMITTED SHOWING THAT THE ACCIDENT ACTUALLY OCCURRED ON DEFENDANT'S PROPERTY. JERROLD AVENUE IS A PUBLIC THOROUGHFARE AND THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT USED THE STREET'S PARKING DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THE NECESSARY CONTROL. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THE SPECIAL RISK EXCEPTION APPLIES. NO SHOWING THAT THE "DANGER IS ONE TO WHICH THE EMPLOYEE, BY REASON OF AND IN CONNECTION WITH HIS EMPLOYMENT, IS SUBJECTED PARTICULARLY OR TO AN ABNORMAL DEGREE. NO RISK IDENTIFIED THAT IS "DISTINCTIVE IN NATURE OR QUANTITATIVELY GREATER THAN RISKS COMMON TO THE PUBLIC." GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA V. WORKERS' COMP. APPEALS BOARD (1976) 16 CAL.3D 595. The Court sustained plaintiff's objections to Stanghellini and Velez declarations, remaining objections overruled. Defendant's objections all overruled as to undisputed facts. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
